codeReview.summary: The fix commit stabilizes one flaky test fixture by switching to a proven text pattern, but it does not remediate the blocking issues called out in the prior failed review for m3-fix-true-incremental-chunk-persistence. The original failure was about production incremental-persistence semantics; this commit changes only test data/comments in ProjectionPipelineServiceTests.

issues.description: Commit 415399b only updates the test fixture (repeating phrase/suffix and assertion messaging) for test_incrementalChunkPersistence_partialTextChange_minimalWrites. It does not modify the production files implicated in the prior failure (ProjectionPipelineService.swift and SearchIndexStore.swift in review m3-fix-true-incremental-chunk-persistence), so this fix alone is insufficient to claim the original failure is resolved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill adherence reporting appears inaccurate for this worker run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: indexing-efficiency-worker requires reading mission.md, AGENTS.md, architecture.md, and user-testing.md in step 1, but transcript grep for session 1535ccca-d9a1-4c81-8e43-bc44562d51e9 shows mission.md and services.yaml reads only (no AGENTS.md, architecture.md, or user-testing.md), while handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

summary: Fail. The reviewed fix improves determinism of one minimal-write test, but based on the two reviewed diffs it does not address the original prior-review blocking findings, which were in production incremental-persistence paths rather than this test fixture.
